active_ingredients: Benzocaine 10% to 20% (oral topical gel/liquid), e.g. Orajel-type oral pain products
indications_and_usage: Uses: for the temporary relief of pain associated with a toothache, sore or irritated gums, minor irritation of the mouth and gums from dentures or orthodontic appliances, canker sores, and pain from minor dental procedures. Works as a local anesthetic numbing the surface where applied.
contraindications: Do not use if you have a history of allergy to benzocaine or other 'caine' local anesthetics, or to PABA.
warnings_and_cautions: Methemoglobinemia warning: use of benzocaine, especially in children under 2 years and in higher doses, has rarely caused methemoglobinemia, a serious blood condition (signs: pale, gray, or blue-colored skin, lips, or nail beds; shortness of breath; headache; fatigue) - seek immediate medical attention if these occur. Do not use in children under 2 years except on the advice of a dentist or doctor. For temporary relief only; see a dentist promptly for a toothache - it usually signals a problem (cavity, infection) that needs treatment. Do not use for more than 7 days unless directed by a dentist or doctor.
adverse_reactions: Local stinging or burning, taste changes; rarely allergic contact reactions or methemoglobinemia.
drug_interactions: No clinically important interactions at recommended topical doses.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 2 years and over: apply a small amount of gel to the affected area up to 4 times daily or as directed by a dentist or doctor; for canker sores, allow to remain in place at least 1 minute, then spit out. Children under 12 should be supervised. Children under 2 years: do not use unless directed by a dentist or doctor.
overdosage: Swallowing large amounts can cause methemoglobinemia or local numbness of the throat; seek medical help or contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
